frameworks.name: E/I balance
frameworks.one_line: cortical dynamics indexing excitatory/inhibitory synaptic alterations

frameworks.name: pharmacological challenge mapping
frameworks.one_line: compare patient maps to NMDA and GABA-A manipulations in healthy subjects

frameworks.name: MEG cortical dynamics
frameworks.one_line: whole-cortex MEG signatures of local population activity

core_question: Can large-scale MEG dynamics reveal distributed synaptic alterations underlying early-stage psychosis?
key_assumptions: local cortical population dynamics reflect underlying E/I synaptic state
psychosis pathology is spatially distributed rather than focal
pharmacological NMDA/GABA-A manipulation produces dynamics signatures comparable to disease
manipulations: clinical group (first-episode psychosis vs clinical high risk vs controls)
pharmacological NMDA receptor manipulation in healthy participants
pharmacological GABA-A receptor manipulation in healthy participants
key_findings: spatial patterns of altered cortical dynamics similar in first-episode and clinical high-risk groups
patient dynamics maps resemble those induced by NMDA and GABA-A pharmacological manipulations
individual symptom variation tracks individual differences in cortical dynamics maps
interpretations: distributed E/I synaptic alterations underlie early psychosis and are visible as MEG dynamics fingerprints
MEG-based dynamical fingerprints offer a candidate biomarker for early detection and individualized assessment
